title: Tattershall (2) Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 4 killed including pilot Flg Off. Ronald William Wright

external_narratives.summary: Heritage record for the crash site of Lancaster PB422 in the River Bain between Tattershall and Coningsby. The aircraft's port outer engine caught fire during takeoff for a bombing exercise, causing it to stall and crash into trees.
external_narratives.key_details: Monument reference MLI125800. Crash occurred on 13 July 1945, two months after VE Day. Four crew killed: Flt Lt R.W. Wright, F/Sgt A.J. Molyneux, Fg Off J.L. Bendix, F/Sgt J. Eastwood.

external_narratives.summary: Accident database entry for Avro Lancaster Mk III PB422 of 97 Squadron, recording the crash on Friday 13 July 1945 near Tattershall during a practice bombing exercise from RAF Coningsby.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft designated OF-P. Port outer engine fire on takeoff caused stall. Aircraft crashed into trees and came to rest in the River Bain.

external_narratives.summary: Registry entry for the combined memorial plaque on Butt's Bridge at Coningsby, commemorating the crews of both Lancaster W4175 and Lancaster PB422 of 97 Squadron.
external_narratives.key_details: Shared memorial with the W4175 crew. Both were 97 Squadron aircraft that crashed in the Tattershall area during different phases of the war.

local_contacts.org_name: Coningsby, Tattershall & District Royal British Legion

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Postcodes.io — Tattershall Lincolnshire LN4 area at 53.090 -0.215
